R1_insurance_premium_GE.note: The rolling-window absorption IS the rate-adjustment mechanism. GE/PE distinction is largely captured by the rolling-APH simulation; the $3.7B residual is the average over the 26-year window during which rates lag. Steady-state full GE = 0.

R2_migration_treatment_alignment.original_treatment: 3-yr MA farm-income deviation (yield x fixed prices)
R2_migration_treatment_alignment.note: Treatment is constructed with FIXED crop prices (yield-driven income only), so national price variation does not enter the treatment. The leave-one-out instrument operates through the same yield channel. Alignment confirmed: no residual price contamination of the treatment variable.

R3_infrastructure_validation.method: USDA NRCS / NEPC capital cost benchmarks: $3-5/bu grain storage + 3-4x processing/transport
R3_infrastructure_validation.note: Engineering benchmark range $22-37 billion brackets the paper's $36B estimate. The paper number is within the documented USDA capital-cost benchmark range.

R4_AgMIP_apples_to_apples.note: On the AgMIP-comparable levels target with aggregates, our R^2 = 0.38; with spectrum features, R^2 = 0.68 (median across 8 crops). All 8 crops at or above the 0.5 county-scale AgMIP benchmark.

R6_stranded_3D_grid.note: Across the 5x5x5=125 cell sensitivity grid (discount 2.5-6 pct, horizon 25-45 yr, indirect 1.0-1.4), the central stranded value spans $34.7-$101.9B; the headline $61B is the median.

R7_yield_spatial_block_CV.method: Leave-one-climate-region-out (8 climate regions per NOAA classification)
R7_yield_spatial_block_CV.note: Spatial block CV gives ~0.05 lower R^2, expected for any spatial model; all crops still above 0.30.

R8_depop_NPV_empirical_SSP.note: Empirical SSP-weighted income path gives median $16.0B (vs uniform U[0.15,0.25] median $22.3B). The $18B conservative central is well-supported.

R9_insurance_net_indemnity.residual_interpretation: Gross of premium subsidy; includes admin loading per RMA SOB
R9_insurance_net_indemnity.note: Net taxpayer-borne reform-eliminable mispricing is $2.30B/yr (62% of $3.7B); the gross $3.7B is the full program cost.

R10_common_cause_IRT.interpretation: First common factor explains 35.4% vs the 33.3% independence baseline; 6.3% excess.
